steps.body: A compressed or stretched spring stores **elastic potential energy**: **U_s = ½kx²**, where **k** is the spring constant and **x** is compression or stretch from natural length.

steps.prompt: Same spring, now compressed **x = 0.6 m**. Drag **U_s**.
hints: U_s grows with x² — doubling x quadruples energy.

steps.prompt: The spring (**k = 200 N/m**) was compressed **0.4 m** and launches a block (no friction, spring only). Drag the **ΔKE** gained by the block.
hints: Spring PE converts to KE (if no losses).
ΔKE = U_s,initial = 16 J from the 0.4 m compression.

steps.title: Lesson complete!
steps.body: You sized spring energy and watched it transfer to kinetic energy.
